summary: PRIMARY BUG FIX CONFIRMED: unlock succeeded immediately with the CORRECTED code 'AGEN-2026' (with dash) on first attempt - typed code into #kodeAkses, clicked #btnAktivasi, waited 6s, lock screen disappeared with message '✅ Aktivasi berhasil! Membuka aplikasi...' and dashboard rendered - NO localStorage bypass was needed this time. This proves the previous 4 testing rounds' failures were purely due to the wrong test credential (AGEN2026 without dash) documented in test_credentials.md, not a code bug, exactly as the user/main agent suspected. Note: after this first successful unlock, the external Apps Script correctly bound the code to that browser/device (1-code-1-device enforced) - subsequent new Playwright browser contexts in later script calls got 'Kode sudah dipakai di perangkat lain' as expected/correct behavior, so remaining verification was done via the documented dev-QA localStorage bypass (network-block script.google.com + set localStorage directly) per test_credentials.md, which is legitimate since the lock mechanism itself was already proven fixed. All 6 previously-implemented visual/functional changes were re-verified and are genuinely present: (1) Header/tabs/Simpan Semua button/Salin Catatan buttons all render in maroon/wine-red (#7c2334 family) - confirmed via screenshots, no blue/green anywhere. (2) Tab Pendapatan: 'Pemilik Usaha' checkbox text absent from page innerText - confirmed removed. (3) Tab Belanja RT: 'Reset Makanan'/'Reset Non-Makanan'/'Reset Tahunan' all absent from innerText while 'Salin Catatan' button is present and visible - confirmed. (4) Tab Usaha 1: exactly 5 separate visible '📋 Salin Catatan' buttons found via DOM query filtering visible elements only - 'Salin Catatan Tenaga Kerja' (in Tenaga Kerja card), 'Salin Catatan 26.b', '26.c', '26.d', '26.e' each directly under its own subtotal box - confirmed NOT a single combined button. (5) Tab Responden #namaResponden input font-family computed as 'Arial' (sans-serif fallback, no Roboto Mono/monospace) - confirmed normal font, not typewriter. (6) Tab Usaha 1 #omzet_1 (Rp field): white background (rgb(255,255,255)), maroon-tinted 'Rp' prefix badge visible in screenshots, dark readable filled value '5.000.000', visible pink/maroon focus border ring on click - confirmed matches spec (font-family is intentionally monospace 'Roboto Mono' for tabular currency alignment per prior round's design decision, this is expected/correct, not the name-field font). No JS console errors or page errors observed throughout all test sequences (only expected ERR_FAILED for the intentionally-blocked script.google.com calls during bypass testing). No React/RN red-screen equivalent or crash observed - this is a vanilla PWA and rendered cleanly throughout.
action_items: No action items - primary bug (wrong test credential) is resolved and confirmed. Direct unlock with 'AGEN-2026' works correctly on a fresh device/browser without needing the localStorage bypass.

Minor observation (not a bug, informational): the Nama Keluarga/Rumah Tangga input placeholder text 'Contoh: NAMASUAMI PER NAMAISTRI' visually clips/truncates within the input width on a 375px mobile viewport before any text is typed - purely a placeholder display quirk, does not affect actual typed input or functionality, optional cosmetic polish only if main agent wants to shorten the placeholder for narrow screens.
critical_code_review_comments: lock.js aktivasiKode(): kode = inputKode.value.trim().toUpperCase() - correctly does NOT strip/normalize dashes or special characters, sends the code verbatim to the Apps Script as previously confirmed by code review; the earlier persistent failures were 100% a wrong-test-credential issue, not a client-side normalization bug.

1-code-1-device enforcement verified working end-to-end in this round: first unlock succeeded, immediate second unlock attempt from a different browser context correctly returned 'Kode sudah dipakai di perangkat lain.' - this is the external Apps Script correctly rejecting a second device for the same code, confirming the feature works as designed (not a bug, but flagged here since it constrains repeatable automated testing - only one successful direct-code unlock is possible per activation cycle on the external backend).

success_rate.backend: N/A - frontend-only scope, no backend APIs in this app
success_rate.frontend: 100% - primary unlock bug fix confirmed working, all 6 previously-implemented visual/functional changes re-verified present and correct, 0 blocking bugs found

seed_data_creation: None persisted - localStorage-based state is per-browser-context; created one throwaway responden ('Budi Santoso Test') during testing to render Usaha 1 tab content, not persisted anywhere durable.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_credentials.md is now CORRECT with 'AGEN-2026' (with dash). Direct unlock works. IMPORTANT for future rounds: the external Apps Script enforces 1-code-1-device, so only the FIRST browser context that unlocks with the real code will stay unlocked; every subsequent Playwright automation call spins a new browser context (new device fingerprint) and will be rejected with 'Kode sudah dipakai di perangkat lain.' This is correct/expected behavior, not a bug. For any UI verification beyond the initial unlock-success screenshot, continue using the documented bypass (block script.google.com + localStorage.setItem('se2026_kode_aktif_v1','AGEN-2026') + reload) within the SAME script call as the rest of the test sequence to avoid re-triggering the device-lock check. To view Usaha 1 tab content, must first: fill #namaResponden, select #jumlahUsaha option '1', then click the floating 'Simpan Semua' button (NOT '+ Responden Baru', which actually RESETS/clears the form for a new blank entry) - this activates respondenAktif and renders the Usaha N tabs.
rca_of_issue: Confirmed root cause of all previous 4 rounds' unlock failures: test_credentials.md contained the wrong code 'AGEN2026' (missing dash) while the real admin-configured code on the external Google Apps Script is 'AGEN-2026' (with dash). lock.js has zero normalization of the typed code (just .trim().toUpperCase()), so a missing dash simply resulted in a legitimate 'kode tidak valid' rejection from the server every time - not a client or server bug. This round's fix (correcting the documented credential) fully resolves the issue; unlock now succeeds on the very first attempt.
